case_title: 100 BUSH CORPORATION VS. FRED G. MEIS et al

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SEVER CROSS COMPLAINT FOR TRIAL FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT SUGARMAN, RANDY SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP HEARING SET FOR JUL-09-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANTS RANDY SUGARMAN AND SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PRACTICING LAW WITHOUT A LAW LICENSE AND SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. MR. MEIS IS GIVEN LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, A COGNIZABLE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS. THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. 100 BUSH CORPORATION'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS FOR THE ISSUANCE OF A RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 484.090(A). DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS NOT PROBABLY VALID. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF 100 BUSH CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY - THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM COHEN. THE MOTIONS ARE GRANTED. THE MOTIONS APPEAR UNOPPOSED. THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ARE DEEMED ADMITTED, WRITTEN RESPONSES TO THE INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION REQUESTS ARE DUE NOT LATER THAN 17TH AUGUST AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE TO BE PRODUCED BY THE 24TH AUGUST. FEE SHIFTING SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1500 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF MOVING PARTY DUE NOT LATER THAN AUGUST 24, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANTS RANDY SUGARMAN, SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 26 TO GIVE MR. MEIS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MR. MEIS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN 4PM OCTOBER 19, OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. MR. MEIS SHOULD ALSO MAKE SURE THAT HIS OPPOSITION PAPERS WERE FILED WITH THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William M. Henley, Esq. of Wild Carey & Fife, Tel. 415.837.3101, for cross defendants and moving party;
Ron Lunski, Esq. of Lunski Law, Tel. 415.426.3525, appearing specially for defendant.

RULING: In light of Mr. Meis' passing, this hearing was set to discuss the status, not the merits, of the motion for summary judgment. The matter was discussed. CROSS DEFENDANTs RANDY SUGARMAN and SUGARMAN & COMPANY LLP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT was taken off calendar by the court. The court also informed the parties that Dept. 206 will send an order vacating the trial date and the case will be set for a case management conference. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

100 Bush Corporation Ntc Of Appl & Hrg For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Oct-30-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen Porter, Esq. of Whitehead & Porter, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling. 100 Bush Corporation's Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment is Granted. Plaintiff has met all of the requirements for the issuance of a Right to Attach Order per Code of Civil Procedure 484.090(a). Defendant has failed to present admissible evidence showing that Plaintiff's claim is not probably valid. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2012 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANTS RANDY SUGARMAN and SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP's DEMURRER TO FRED G. MEIS' 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. Cross-Defendants argue factual issues that are improperly determined on demurrer. The Cross-Complaint is sufficiently pled and states a cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2012 Continued To Oct-26-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANTS RANDY SUGARMAN, SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to October 26 to give Mr. Meis the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Meis must deliver courtesy copies of his opposition papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than 4pm October 19, or the demurrer will be sustained without leave to amend. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Mr. Meis should also make sure that his opposition papers were filed with the Court. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - FRED MEIS, TEL. NO. 981-4612, PETITIONER APPEARING IN COURT;
ATTY. EDWARD J. NEVIN, TEL. NO. (707) 766-9556 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. WILLIAM M. HENLEY OF WILD CAREY & FIFE, TEL. NO. 837-3101 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT;
ATTY. KEVIN M. SULLIVAN OF GRIFFINS & SULLIVAN, TEL. NO. 369-2600 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT LORI GOODELL.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS RANDY SUGARMAN AND SUGARMAN AND COMPANY LLP'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Sustained, without leave to amend as to the cause of action for practicing law without a law license and sustained with fifteen Leave to amend as to the causes of action for breach of contract, professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty. Mr. Meis is given leave to allege, if he can do so in good faith, a cognizable cause of action against the cross-defendants. The court on its own motion granted leave to file cross-complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
